question_text: Which statement best describes what particles are in solids, liquids, and gases?

options.label: A
options.text: Particles are only found in gases; solids and liquids have no particles.

options.label: B
options.text: All matter is made of particles that are always moving, but arranged and moving differently in each state.

options.label: C
options.text: Particles in solids are stationary and don't move at all.

options.label: D
options.text: Particles are much larger in liquids than in gases.

explanation: All states of matter are made of the same particles, but their arrangement and movement differ. In solids, particles vibrate in fixed positions; in liquids, they move around but stay close together; in gases, they move freely and far apart.
